research.interface: Databricks Jobs REST API 2.2 (POST /api/2.2/jobs/runs/submit and related run endpoints)

official_sources.title: w.jobs: Jobs — Databricks SDK for Python documentation
official_sources.used_for: submit() one-time run parameters (tasks, run_name, idempotency_token max 64 chars, timeout_seconds); get_run pagination params; get_run_output limited to the first 5 MB; cancel_run cancels asynchronously so the run may still be running when the call returns

official_sources.title: Updating from Jobs API 2.1 to 2.2 | Databricks on AWS
official_sources.used_for: Jobs API 2.2 is the current version; tasks arrays are capped at 100 elements with next_page_token replacing has_more; Get a single run supports pagination and only_latest

official_sources.title: databricks/databricks-sdk-go service/jobs/impl.go (official Databricks SDK source)
official_sources.used_for: exact 2.2 REST paths and headers: POST /api/2.2/jobs/runs/submit, GET /api/2.2/jobs/runs/get, GET /api/2.2/jobs/runs/get-output, POST /api/2.2/jobs/runs/cancel; Accept: application/json on all, Content-Type: application/json on POSTs

official_sources.title: Jobs dataclasses — Databricks SDK for Python documentation
official_sources.used_for: Run object fields (run_id, status replacing deprecated state, tasks with per-task run_id/task_key/attempt_number, run_page_url, next_page_token); RunOutput fields (notebook_output.result/truncated, error, error_trace, logs, metadata); termination_details {code, type, message}

official_sources.title: databricks/databricks-sdk-java RunLifecycleStateV2State.java (official Databricks SDK source)
official_sources.used_for: status.state enum values: BLOCKED, PENDING, QUEUED, RUNNING, TERMINATED, TERMINATING, WAITING

official_sources.title: databricks/databricks-sdk-java TerminationCodeCode.java (official Databricks SDK source)
official_sources.used_for: termination codes pinned by tests: SUCCESS (completed successfully), RUN_EXECUTION_ERROR (completed with task failures), USER_CANCELED (successfully canceled during execution by a user)

official_sources.title: databricks/databricks-sdk-java RunResultState.java (official Databricks SDK source)
official_sources.used_for: legacy state.result_state enum: SUCCESS, FAILED, CANCELED, TIMEDOUT, SUCCESS_WITH_FAILURES, UPSTREAM_FAILED, UPSTREAM_CANCELED, EXCLUDED, DISABLED, MAXIMUM_CONCURRENT_RUNS_REACHED

research.language_rationale: The batch-orchestration tooling this runner slots into is Python. Databricks' official Python SDK is a thin generated wrapper over these same REST endpoints, and the verify environment is stdlib-only, so urllib.request against the documented Jobs API 2.2 endpoints is the credible, dependency-free integration surface; the REST reference is Databricks' primary documented interface.

verified_facts: Jobs API 2.2 is the current stable version; run submission is POST /api/2.2/jobs/runs/submit and returns only {run_id}.
idempotency_token (max 64 chars) guarantees exactly one run per token: resubmitting after a failure returns the existing run_id instead of launching a second run.

Run status polling is GET /api/2.2/jobs/runs/get?run_id=; the modern status field carries state (BLOCKED/PENDING/QUEUED/RUNNING/TERMINATING/TERMINATED/WAITING) plus termination_details {code, type, message}; the legacy state field is deprecated in 2.2 but still served.
termination_details.type is one of SUCCESS, CLIENT_ERROR, CLOUD_FAILURE, INTERNAL_ERROR; codes include SUCCESS, RUN_EXECUTION_ERROR, USER_CANCELED.
Run output is retrieved per task run: GET /api/2.2/jobs/runs/get-output?run_id=<task run_id>; a multi-task parent run has no output of its own, and only the first 5 MiB are returned.
POST /api/2.2/jobs/runs/cancel cancels asynchronously — callers must keep polling until TERMINATED.
Databricks REST errors use the {error_code, message} envelope; 429 rate limiting carries Retry-After.
Every call requires Authorization: Bearer <token>, Accept: application/json, and JSON POST bodies use Content-Type: application/json.
